thesis_headline: The next-gen AI data center builder -- Applied Digital is constructing purpose-built GPU-optimized facilities for the compute clusters Aschenbrenner says are coming
thesis_summary:
Aschenbrenner's $278M Applied Digital position (5% of portfolio, 11.3M shares) targets yet another AI data center operator, but one with a different profile than Core Scientific or IREN. While those companies are pivoting existing Bitcoin mining infrastructure, Applied Digital is building purpose-built, next-generation data centers designed from the ground up for AI/HPC workloads.

Applied Digital operates in three segments: (1) data center hosting for Bitcoin mining, (2) cloud services providing GPU compute access, and (3) development of large-scale data centers specifically designed for AI training clusters. The company is building multi-hundred-MW campuses with the power density, cooling infrastructure, and networking architecture required for next-generation AI workloads.

For Aschenbrenner's thesis, Applied Digital represents the construction phase of the AGI buildout. While CoreWeave operates the GPU cloud and Core Scientific provides existing capacity, Applied Digital is actively building the next wave of purpose-built AI data centers. The company has announced agreements with major customers for its HPC hosting services and is developing campuses in North Dakota, Texas, and other locations with access to low-cost power.
why_this_price: At $278M and 5% of portfolio, Applied Digital is at the threshold of Aschenbrenner's conviction bets. The position is entirely common stock (11.3M shares at ~$24.52/share implied). Applied Digital is a smaller, higher-risk name compared to Core Scientific or CoreWeave, but with potentially higher upside given its greenfield data center development pipeline. The company's value lies in its development pipeline: if completed and leased to AI compute customers, the campuses under construction could generate significant contracted revenue.

investor_in_their_own_words.quote: Many trillions of dollars will go into GPU, datacenter, and power buildout before the decade is out. The industrial mobilization will be staggering.
The fierce scramble to secure every power contract, every GPU allocation, every data center site has only just begun.

valuation_math.approach: Development pipeline valuation -- value of data centers under construction at completion

steps.label: Data center development
steps.value: Purpose-built AI/HPC data centers under construction
steps.note: Multi-hundred MW campuses designed for high-density GPU compute

steps.label: Power pipeline
steps.value: Securing power in locations with low-cost electricity
steps.note: North Dakota, Texas -- access to cheap power is a competitive advantage

steps.label: Customer agreements
steps.value: Long-term hosting contracts with AI compute customers
steps.note: Contracted revenue provides financing and visibility

valuation_math.implied_return: High if development pipeline is completed and leased successfully

key_metrics.business_model: AI data center development, hosting, and cloud services
key_metrics.locations: North Dakota, Texas, other U.S. sites

moat_sources: Secured power contracts in locations with low-cost electricity
Purpose-built design for AI/HPC workloads (higher power density than legacy data centers)
Growing track record of building and operating data centers
Relationships with GPU cloud providers and AI companies

risks.risk: Construction/development execution risk -- building data centers on time and on budget
risks.mitigation: Experienced development team; contracted customer demand reduces market risk

risks.risk: Capital intensity -- significant upfront investment required before revenue
risks.mitigation: Long-term hosting contracts provide financing basis; capital markets accessible

risks.risk: Smaller company with less operational track record than established operators
risks.mitigation: First-mover advantage in purpose-built AI data centers; experienced management

risks.risk: Customer concentration -- dependent on a few large AI compute customers
risks.mitigation: Market demand exceeds supply; multiple potential customers available

catalysts: Completion and energization of data center campuses under construction
New customer contract announcements for AI/HPC hosting
Revenue acceleration as built capacity comes online and is leased
Power capacity expansion through additional utility agreements
company_brief: Applied Digital Corporation (NASDAQ: APLD) is a data center company designing, building, and operating next-generation digital infrastructure for AI, HPC, and Bitcoin mining applications. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, the company operates in three segments: data center hosting (providing physical space, power, and cooling for compute equipment), cloud services (GPU compute access), and HPC data center development. Applied Digital is actively constructing purpose-built campuses designed specifically for high-density AI workloads -- facilities with the power density, liquid cooling, and networking infrastructure that next-generation GPU clusters require. The company's development pipeline spans multiple U.S. locations with access to low-cost power. For Aschenbrenner, Applied Digital represents the construction phase of the AGI infrastructure buildout -- the company is building the physical facilities where the 'hundreds of millions of GPUs' he predicts will be deployed.
